ddls:
CREATE TABLE "lookup_operations" (
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each lookup operation */,
  "patch" INTEGER /* Patch number for the operation */,
  "offset" INTEGER /* Offset value used in the operation */,
  "count_ms" INTEGER /* Time taken for the operation in milliseconds */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the operation occurred */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm used for the lookup */,
  "input_size" INTEGER /* Size of the input data for the lookup */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the lookup was performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the operation (e.g., success, failure, pending) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lookup_metrics" (
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metric record */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated lookup operation */,
  "lookup_time" INTEGER /* Time taken for the lookup in microseconds */,
  "results_returned" INTEGER /* Number of results returned by the lookup */,
  "lookup_type" TEXT /* Type of lookup (e.g., MUS-1, MUS-50, MUS-100, MUS-inf) */,
  "input_size" INTEGER /* Size of the input data for the lookup */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the lookup was performed */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm used for the lookup */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the lookup failed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metric_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lookup_metrics_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES lookup_operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "performance_results" (
  "result_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each result record */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated lookup operation */,
  "results_1" INTEGER /* Result count for the first lookup type */,
  "results_50" INTEGER /* Result count for the second lookup type */,
  "results_100" INTEGER /* Result count for the third lookup type */,
  "results_inf" INTEGER /* Result count for the infinite lookup type */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm used for the lookup */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the lookup was performed */,
  "input_size" INTEGER /* Size of the input data for the lookup */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the lookup failed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("result_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_performance_results_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES lookup_operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., data analyst, engineer, manager) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for user authentication */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the user's last login */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user account is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user accessing the system */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the operation being accessed */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Time when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Action taken by the user (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the user accessed the system */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the client accessing the system */,
  "is_success" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the access was successful */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES lookup_operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lookup_types" (
  "lookup_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each lookup type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the lookup type (e.g., MUS-1, MUS-50) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the lookup type */,
  "is_default" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the lookup type is the default type */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lookup_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "system_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each setting */,
  "setting_name" TEXT /* Name of the setting */,
  "setting_value" TEXT /* Value of the setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the setting */,
  "is_editable" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the setting is editable */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lookup_thresholds" (
  "threshold_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each threshold entry */,
  "lookup_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of lookup */,
  "warning_threshold" INTEGER /* Warning threshold for performance */,
  "error_threshold" INTEGER /* Error threshold for performance */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the threshold is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the threshold was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the threshold was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("threshold_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lookup_thresholds_lookup_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("lookup_type_id") REFERENCES lookup_types ("lookup_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lookup_histories" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each history record */,
  "operation_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated lookup operation */,
  "lookup_time" INTEGER /* Time taken for the lookup in microseconds */,
  "results_returned" INTEGER /* Number of results returned by the lookup */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when the history record was created */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm used for the lookup */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the lookup was performed */,
  "input_size" INTEGER /* Size of the input data for the lookup */,
  "error_message" TEXT /* Error message if the lookup failed */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lookup_histories_operation_id FOREIGN KEY ("operation_id") REFERENCES lookup_operations ("operation_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "sent_time" TEXT /* Time when the notification was sent */,
  "is_read" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the notification has been read */,
  "is_important" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the notification is important */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "lookup_statistics" (
  "stat_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistical record */,
  "lookup_type_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the type of lookup */,
  "average_time" REAL /* Average time taken for lookups */,
  "success_rate" REAL /* Success rate of the lookups */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp when statistics were computed */,
  "algorithm_id" INTEGER /* ID of the algorithm used for the lookup */,
  "environment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the environment where the lookup was performed */,
  "input_size" INTEGER /* Size of the input data for the lookup */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("stat_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_lookup_statistics_lookup_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("lookup_type_id") REFERENCES lookup_types ("lookup_type_id")
)
